mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains below trailing CPIAUCSL-implied inflation (real policy rate negative), the low real cost of capital supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days on average.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return during negative real-rate periods is not significantly greater than the unconditional sample mean (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window historically precedes broad-based earnings downgrades and risk-off repositioning, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the average triggered return is not significantly below the unconditional average (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) growth and HOUST (housing starts) growth signals a synchronized consumer and housing demand slowdown that is not captured by either series alone, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as growth expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3m annualized growth < 0 AND HOUST 3m annualized growth < -5%
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes produce non-negative average 20-day forward returns, or the interaction signal's predictive power is not superior to either single-series signal at p > 0.05 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, positive INDPRO growth signals capacity utilization pressure and margin compression risk rather than fresh demand strength, so the historically positive INDPRO-return relationship flips to negative or neutral, reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO YoY growth > 2% while GDPC1 YoY growth is decelerating for 2+ consecutive quarters
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with decelerating GDP growth
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following the trigger remain positive and statistically indistinguishable from early-cycle INDPRO-positive periods (p > 0.05), indicating no regime-dependent sign flip.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in FEDFUNDS exceeding 25 basis points within a single month signals an accelerated tightening pace that spikes short-term rate volatility expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets reprice discount rates.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25pp within one month
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns after triggered hikes are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) or positive in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in PAYEMS reflect ongoing labor market strength that supports consumer income and near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing modestly positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: Rolling correlation between PAYEMS monthly change sign and 5-day forward return sign falls below 0.05 over any 2-year window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: In an early-to-mid cycle expansion regime, positive GDPC1 growth combined with rising RSAFS signals durable demand strength that supports S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter, but this relationship reverses or weakens once the cycle matures and inflation risk dominates investor concerns.
failure_condition: Positive joint GDPC1/RSAFS growth periods show no significant positive skew in 60-day forward returns relative to negative-growth periods (p > 0.05) within the identified regime.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of low FEDFUNDS combined with rising UNRATE indicates the Fed is behind the curve on supporting a weakening labor market, creating uncertainty that suppresses S&P 500 returns over the next month until clearer easing signals emerge.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 2% AND UNRATE rising for 2+ consecutive months
regime_hint: policy lag / early labor market deterioration
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during joint-trigger episodes are not significantly negative relative to baseline (p > 0.05), or the joint signal underperforms the UNRATE-only threshold signal in walk-forward comparison.
